commentary: Survey digital adoption is directly comparable to synthetic digital tool usage.
Synthetic technology score includes digital tools and payment reliability, so exact agreement is not expected.
Survey measures perceived pain; synthetic variable measures payout delay days.
Cashflow pain is compared with synthetic working-capital pressure.
Primary credit need is not the same as synthetic loan offer; compare directionally.
Compares requested/needed credit size with positive synthetic loan amounts.
Primary perceived growth is aligned with synthetic advancement score.
Perceived growth is compared with synthetic income growth after percentile scaling.
The uploaded survey does not contain explicit retention intent; repeat-customer change is used as a lock-in proxy.
Repeat-customer improvement is only a weak proxy for actual provider retention.
Primary AI usage maps directly to synthetic agent usage flag.
Forecast usage is narrower than general AI-tool use.
Primary AI use is compared with the broader agentic-intelligence score.
